sn1.41:1.2: that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
sn1.41:1.3: Then a certain devatā, in the far extreme of the night, her extreme radiance lighting up the entirety of Jeta’s Grove, went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, having bowed down to him, stood to one side.
sn1.41:1.4: As she was standing there, she recited these verses in the Blessed One’s presence:
sn1.41:2.3: is the one that will be of use,
sn1.41:2.4: not the one left there to burn.
sn1.41:3.1: So when the world is on fire
sn1.41:3.3: one should salvage (one’s wealth) by giving:
sn1.41:4.1: What’s given bears fruit as pleasure.
sn1.41:4.3: thieves take it away, or kings;
sn1.41:4.4: it gets burnt by fire or lost.
sn1.41:5.1: Then in the end one leaves the body
sn1.41:5.5: Having enjoyed & given in line with his means,
sn1.41:5.6: uncensured he goes to the heavenly state.”
